Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Finally, from 8 to 10 seconds, the child lowers their head, reducing glare, while the TV transitions to abstract green geometric visuals.

From 14 to 16 seconds, the television content shifts again, displaying a figure lying on a white bed after previously showing a person in a hat. The word "Dancing" appears in the upper left corner of the screen. Throughout this interval, the blonde child remains in the foreground facing forward, consistently illuminated by strong backlighting from the window, with no significant change in their posture or orientation.

From 16 to 18 seconds, the television transitions from the bed scene to an exterior view of a multi-story building with a figure on a balcony, while the child's mouth opens slightly. Between 18 and 20 seconds, the screen shifts to a figure on a green surface, then a close-up face with subtitles. Simultaneously, the child's expression changes from wide-eyed surprise to a smile, maintaining their position under strong backlighting.

From 20 to 22 seconds, the child closes their eyes while the TV shifts to white text on a dark background. Between 22 and 24 seconds, the child opens their eyes as the screen shows a person in a vest, then a large building. From 24 to 26 seconds, the display changes to a figure on a dock near water. Through 38 seconds, the child keeps both hands over their face as the television cycles through scenes of a person on a dock, a close-up face, and a blue-green grid pattern.

From 38 to 42 seconds, the child maintains both hands pressed firmly over their mouth and nose area without changing posture. Meanwhile, the television screen transitions from the previously seen blue-green grid pattern to a close-up shot of a person's head set against a light background. Throughout this final interval, the strong natural backlighting from the window on the right remains constant, continuing to illuminate the child's blonde hair while washing out finer details.

queries.question: Describe the sequential actions of the child and the changing content on the television screen throughout the clip.

answers.text: From 0 to 2 seconds, a dark silhouette sits before a TV showing a seated person. Between 2 and 4 seconds, the figure is revealed as a blonde child facing forward while the screen shifts to blue graphics. At 4 seconds, the TV displays a palm tree scene with text. By 6 seconds, the screen shows a person lying down as light intensifies. Finally, from 8 to 10 seconds, the child lowers their head, reducing glare, while the TV transitions to abstract green geometric visuals.

From 14 to 16 seconds, the television content shifts again, displaying a figure lying on a white bed after previously showing a person in a hat. The word "Dancing" appears in the upper left corner of the screen. Throughout this interval, the blonde child remains in the foreground facing forward, consistently illuminated by strong backlighting from the window, with no significant change in their posture or orientation.

From 16 to 18 seconds, the television transitions from the bed scene to an exterior view of a multi-story building with a figure on a balcony, while the child's mouth opens slightly. Between 18 and 20 seconds, the screen shifts to a figure on a green surface, then a close-up face with subtitles. Simultaneously, the child's expression changes from wide-eyed surprise to a smile, maintaining their position under strong backlighting.

From 20 to 22 seconds, the child closes their eyes while the TV shifts to white text on a dark background. Between 22 and 24 seconds, the child opens their eyes as the screen shows a person in a vest, then a large building. From 24 to 26 seconds, the display changes to a figure on a dock near water. Finally, at 26 to 28 seconds, the scene transitions to a person with subtitles, then a bridge, while the child closes their mouth.

From 28 to 30 seconds, the child raises their right hand to cover their mouth as the TV shows white text. Between 30 and 32 seconds, they add their left hand, covering the nose, while the screen shifts to a building exterior. Through 38 seconds, the child keeps both hands over their face as the television cycles through scenes of a person on a dock, a close-up face, and a blue-green grid pattern.

From 38 to 42 seconds, the child maintains both hands pressed firmly over their mouth and nose area without changing posture. Meanwhile, the television screen transitions from the previously seen blue-green grid pattern to a close-up shot of a person's head set against a light background. Throughout this final interval, the strong natural backlighting from the window on the right remains constant, continuing to illuminate the child's blonde hair while washing out finer details.

queries.question: Describe the sequential actions of the child and the changing content on the television screen throughout the clip.

answers.text: From 0 to 2 seconds, a dark silhouette sits before a TV showing a seated person. Between 2 and 4 seconds, the figure is revealed as a blonde child facing forward while the screen shifts to blue graphics. At 4 seconds, the TV displays a palm tree scene with text. By 6 seconds, the screen shows a person lying down as light intensifies. Finally, from 8 to 10 seconds, the child lowers their head, reducing glare, while the TV transitions to abstract green geometric visuals.

From 14 to 16 seconds, the television content shifts again, displaying a figure lying on a white bed after previously showing a person in a hat. The word "Dancing" appears in the upper left corner of the screen. Throughout this interval, the blonde child remains in the foreground facing forward, consistently illuminated by strong backlighting from the window, with no significant change in their posture or orientation.

From 16 to 18 seconds, the television transitions from the bed scene to an exterior view of a multi-story building with a figure on a balcony, while the child's mouth opens slightly. Between 18 and 20 seconds, the screen shifts to a figure on a green surface, then a close-up face with subtitles. Simultaneously, the child's expression changes from wide-eyed surprise to a smile, maintaining their position under strong backlighting.

From 20 to 22 seconds, the child closes their eyes while the TV shifts to white text on a dark background. Between 22 and 24 seconds, the child opens their eyes as the screen shows a person in a vest, then a large building. From 24 to 26 seconds, the display changes to a figure on a dock near water. Finally, at 26 to 28 seconds, the scene transitions to a person with subtitles, then a bridge, while the child closes their mouth.

From 28 to 30 seconds, the child raises their right hand to cover their mouth as the TV shows white text. Between 30 and 32 seconds, they add their left hand, covering the nose, while the screen shifts to a building exterior. Through 38 seconds, the child keeps both hands over their face as the television cycles through scenes of a person on a dock, a close-up face, and a blue-green grid pattern.

From 38 to 42 seconds, the child maintains both hands pressed firmly over their mouth and nose area without changing posture. Meanwhile, the television screen transitions from the previously seen blue-green grid pattern to a close-up shot of a person's head set against a light background. Throughout this final interval, the strong natural backlighting from the window on the right remains constant, continuing to illuminate the child's blonde hair while washing out finer details.

queries.question: Describe the sequential actions of the child and the changing content on the television screen throughout the clip.

answers.text: From 0 to 2 seconds, a dark silhouette sits before a TV showing a seated person. Between 2 and 4 seconds, the figure is revealed as a blonde child facing forward while the screen shifts to blue graphics. At 4 seconds, the TV displays a palm tree scene with text. By 6 seconds, the screen shows a person lying down as light intensifies. Finally, from 8 to 10 seconds, the child lowers their head, reducing glare, while the TV transitions to abstract green geometric visuals.

From 14 to 16 seconds, the television content shifts again, displaying a figure lying on a white bed after previously showing a person in a hat. The word "Dancing" appears in the upper left corner of the screen. Throughout this interval, the blonde child remains in the foreground facing forward, consistently illuminated by strong backlighting from the window, with no significant change in their posture or orientation.

From 16 to 18 seconds, the television transitions from the bed scene to an exterior view of a multi-story building with a figure on a balcony, while the child's mouth opens slightly. Between 18 and 20 seconds, the screen shifts to a figure on a green surface, then a close-up face with subtitles. Simultaneously, the child's expression changes from wide-eyed surprise to a smile, maintaining their position under strong backlighting.

From 20 to 22 seconds, the child closes their eyes while the TV shifts to white text on a dark background. Between 22 and 24 seconds, the child opens their eyes as the screen shows a person in a vest, then a large building. From 24 to 26 seconds, the display changes to a figure on a dock near water. Finally, at 26 to 28 seconds, the scene transitions to a person with subtitles, then a bridge, while the child closes their mouth.

From 28 to 30 seconds, the child raises their right hand to cover their mouth as the TV shows white text. Between 30 and 32 seconds, they add their left hand, covering the nose, while the screen shifts to a building exterior. Through 38 seconds, the child keeps both hands over their face as the television cycles through scenes of a person on a dock, a close-up face, and a blue-green grid pattern.

From 38 to 42 seconds, the child maintains both hands pressed firmly over their mouth and nose area without changing posture. Meanwhile, the television screen transitions from the previously seen blue-green grid pattern to a close-up shot of a person's head set against a light background. Throughout this final interval, the strong natural backlighting from the window on the right remains constant, continuing to illuminate the child's blonde hair while washing out finer details.
